full_response:
Step-by-step derivation

1. Definition of 4-velocity  
   U^μ ≡ dx^μ / dτ , μ = 0,1,2,3
   where τ is the particle’s proper time.

2. Split into temporal and spatial parts  
   • Temporal component: U^0 = c dt/dτ  
   • Spatial components: U^i = dx^i/dτ , i = 1,2,3

3. Relate proper time to coordinate time  
   By definition of the Lorentz factor γ,
        dτ = dt / γ  ⟺ γ = dt/dτ .

4. Express U^0 in terms of γ  
        U^0 = c dt/dτ = γ c .

5. Express U^i in terms of the ordinary 3-velocity v^i = dx^i/dt  
        U^i = dx^i/dτ = (dx^i/dt)(dt/dτ) = v^i γ .

6. Eliminate γ between the last two relations  
        v^i = U^i / γ = U^i /(U^0 / c) = c U^i / U^0 .

Thus the three–velocity components in the given frame are obtained directly from the spatial and temporal components of U^μ.

\boxed{\,v^{\,i} = c\,\dfrac{U^{i}}{U^{0}}\,,\qquad i = 1,2,3\,}

Confidence: 93%
